hypothesis: Incorporating physics-informed coordination-chemistry constraints and explicit charge representations into a uMLIP trained on a multi-fidelity MOF dataset that includes non-equilibrium configurations will improve zero-shot MOF predictions, yielding MD stability over 50 ps at 300 K and bulk modulus/heat capacity predictions within 5% of DFT for MOFs with previously unseen metal nodes, outperforming unconstrained uMLIPs.
